content.content:
1→-- 001_initial_schema.sql
     2→-- Creates all tables for the Mirrorless application
     3→
     4→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "pgcrypto";
     5→
     6→CREATE TABLE users (
     7→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
     8→  name text NOT NULL,
     9→  email text UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    10→  phone text,
    11→  google_oauth_token jsonb,
    12→  poke_id text,
    13→  created_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
    14→);
    15→
    16→CREATE TABLE style_profiles (
    17→  user_id uuid PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    18→  brands text[] DEFAULT '{}',
    19→  price_range jsonb,
    20→  style_tags text[] DEFAULT '{}',
    21→  size_info jsonb,
    22→  narrative_summary text
    23→);
    24→
    25→CREATE TABLE purchases (
    26→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    27→  user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    28→  brand text NOT NULL,
    29→  item_name text NOT NULL,
    30→  category text,
    31→  price numeric,
    32→  date date,
    33→  source_email_id text
    34→);
    35→
    36→CREATE TABLE sessions (
    37→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    38→  user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    39→  started_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    40→  ended_at timestamptz,
    41→  status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active' CHECK (status IN ('active', 'completed', 'abandoned'))
    42→);
    43→
    44→CREATE TABLE clothing_items (
    45→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    46→  name text NOT NULL,
    47→  brand text,
    48→  price numeric,
    49→  image_url text,
    50→  buy_url text,
    51→  category text,
    52→  source text DEFAULT 'serpapi'
    53→);
    54→
    55→CREATE TABLE session_outfits (
    56→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    57→  session_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    58→  outfit_data jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
    59→  reaction text CHECK (reaction IN ('liked', 'disliked', 'skipped')),
    60→  clothing_items uuid[] DEFAULT '{}'
    61→);
    62→
    63→CREATE TABLE queue (
    64→  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    65→  user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    66→  position integer NOT NULL,
    67→  status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'waiting' CHECK (status IN ('waiting', 'active', 'completed')),
    68→  joined_at timestamptz NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
    69→);
    70→
    71→-- Indexes
    72→CREATE INDEX idx_purchases_user_id ON purchases(user_id);
    73→CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_user_id ON sessions(user_id);
    74→CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_status ON sessions(status);
    75→CREATE INDEX idx_session_outfits_session_id ON session_outfits(session_id);
    76→CREATE INDEX idx_queue_status ON queue(status);
    77→CREATE INDEX idx_queue_position ON queue(position);
    78→

1→-- 002_enrich_purchases.sql
     2→-- Adds richer purchase data for AI agent consumption and prevents duplicate imports.
     3→
     4→ALTER TABLE purchases
     5→  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS merchant text,
     6→  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS order_status text,
     7→  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS tracking_number text,
     8→  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS receipt_text text;
     9→
    10→-- Prevent duplicate imports from the same email + item combination.
    11→-- Uses COALESCE so rows with NULL source_email_id don't collide.
    12→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_purchases_email_item
    13→  ON purchases(user_id, COALESCE(source_email_id, ''), item_name);
    14→

1→"""Google OAuth token exchange and Gmail API service builder."""
     2→
     3→import os
     4→
     5→from google.auth.transport.requests import Request
     6→from google.oauth2.credentials import Credentials
     7→from google_auth_oauthlib.flow import InstalledAppFlow
     8→from googleapiclient.discovery import build
     9→
    10→SCOPES = [
    11→    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly",
    12→    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/userinfo.profile",
    13→    "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/userinfo.email",
    14→]
    15→
    16→
    17→def exchange_auth_code(auth_code: str, redirect_uri: str) -> dict:
    18→    """Exchange a Google OAuth authorization code for tokens."""
    19→    client_id = os.getenv("GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID")
    20→    client_secret = os.getenv("GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET")
    21→    if not client_id or not client_secret:
    22→        raise ValueError("GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables must be set")
    23→    client_config = {
    24→        "web": {
    25→            "client_id": client_id,
    26→            "client_secret": client_secret,
    27→            "auth_uri": "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth",
    28→            "token_uri": "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token",
    29→            "redirect_uris": [redirect_uri],
    30→        }
    31→    }
    32→    flow = InstalledAppFlow.from_client_config(client_config, scopes=SCOPES)
    33→    flow.redirect_uri = redirect_uri
    34→    flow.fetch_token(code=auth_code)
    35→
    36→    creds = flow.credentials
    37→    return {
    38→        "access_token": creds.token,
    39→        "refresh_token": creds.refresh_token,
    40→        "token_uri": creds.token_uri,
    41→        "client_id": creds.client_id,
    42→        "client_secret": creds.client_secret,
    43→    }
    44→
    45→
    46→def build_gmail_service(token_data: dict):
    47→    """Build an authenticated Gmail API service from stored token data."""
    48→    creds = Credentials(
    49→        token=token_data["access_token"],
    50→        refresh_token=token_data.get("refresh_token"),
    51→        token_uri=token_data.get("token_uri", "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"),
    52→        client_id=token_data.get("client_id"),
    53→        client_secret=token_data.get("client_secret"),
    54→    )
    55→    if creds.expired and creds.refresh_token:
    56→        creds.refresh(Request())
    57→    return build("gmail", "v1", credentials=creds)
    58→

1→"""Gmail message search and content extraction."""
     2→
     3→import base64
     4→from email.utils import parsedate_to_datetime
     5→
     6→
     7→def search_emails(service, query: str, max_results: int = 10) -> list[str]:
     8→    """Search Gmail for messages matching query. Returns list of message IDs."""
     9→    response = (
    10→        service.users()
    11→        .messages()
    12→        .list(userId="me", q=query, maxResults=max_results)
    13→        .execute()
    14→    )
    15→    messages = response.get("messages", [])
    16→    return [m["id"] for m in messages]
    17→
    18→
    19→def get_message_content(service, message_id: str) -> dict:
    20→    """Fetch a single Gmail message and extract subject, sender, date, body."""
    21→    msg = (
    22→        service.users()
    23→        .messages()
    24→        .get(userId="me", id=message_id, format="full")
    25→        .execute()
    26→    )
    27→    payload = msg.get("payload", {})
    28→    headers = {h["name"]: h["value"] for h in payload.get("headers", [])}
    29→
    30→    body_text = _extract_body(payload)
    31→
    32→    date_str = headers.get("Date", "")
    33→    parsed_date = None
    34→    if date_str:
    35→        try:
    36→            parsed_date = parsedate_to_datetime(date_str).isoformat()
    37→        except Exception:
    38→            parsed_date = date_str
    39→
    40→    return {
    41→        "message_id": message_id,
    42→        "subject": headers.get("Subject", ""),
    43→        "sender": headers.get("From", ""),
    44→        "date": parsed_date,
    45→        "body": body_text,
    46→    }
    47→
    48→
    49→def _find_mime_part(payload: dict, mime_type: str) -> str | None:
    50→    """Recursively search MIME parts for the first part matching mime_type.
    51→
    52→    Returns decoded text content or None if not found.
    53→    """
    54→    if payload.get("mimeType") == mime_type and "body" in payload:
    55→        data = payload["body"].get("data", "")
    56→        if data:
    57→            return base64.urlsafe_b64decode(data).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
    58→
    59→    for part in payload.get("parts", []):
    60→        result = _find_mime_part(part, mime_type)
    61→        if result:
    62→            return result
    63→
    64→    return None
    65→
    66→
    67→def _extract_body(payload: dict) -> str:
    68→    """Extract text body from Gmail message payload.
    69→
    70→    Strategy: prefer text/plain, fall back to text/html converted via BeautifulSoup.
    71→    """
    72→    plain = _find_mime_part(payload, "text/plain")
    73→    if plain:
    74→        return plain
    75→
    76→    html = _find_mime_part(payload, "text/html")
    77→    if html:
    78→        from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
    79→
    80→        soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    81→        for tag in soup(["script", "style"]):
    82→            tag.decompose()
    83→        return soup.get_text(separator="\n", strip=True)
    84→
    85→    return ""
    86→

1→"""Extract purchase data from receipt emails using pattern matching + LLM fallback."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→import logging
     5→import os
     6→import re
     7→from datetime import date
     8→
     9→import anthropic
    10→
    11→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    12→
    13→# Known receipt sender patterns (lowercase substrings)
    14→RECEIPT_SENDERS = [
    15→    "amazon.com",
    16→    "noreply@zara.com",
    17→    "nordstrom.com",
    18→    "nike.com",
    19→    "uniqlo.com",
    20→    "hm.com",
    21→    "aritzia.com",
    22→    "gap.com",
    23→    "macys.com",
    24→    "urbanoutfitters.com",
    25→    "asos.com",
    26→    "ssense.com",
    27→    "farfetch.com",
    28→    "net-a-porter.com",
    29→    "shopify.com",
    30→    "etsy.com",
    31→    "ebay.com",
    32→    "stockx.com",
    33→    "goat.com",
    34→    "depop.com",
    35→    "poshmark.com",
    36→    "grailed.com",
    37→    "thredup.com",
    38→    "mrporter.com",
    39→    "revolve.com",
    40→    "shein.com",
    41→    "target.com",
    42→    "walmart.com",
    43→    "chewy.com",
    44→    "store-news@",
    45→    "order-update@",
    46→    "receipt@",
    47→    "confirmation@",
    48→]
    49→
    50→# Known brand names for matching
    51→KNOWN_BRANDS = [
    52→    "Nike", "Adidas", "Zara", "H&M", "Uniqlo", "Aritzia", "Nordstrom",
    53→    "Gap", "Levi's", "Patagonia", "The North Face", "Lululemon",
    54→    "Gucci", "Prada", "Balenciaga", "Supreme", "Off-White", "Stussy",
    55→    "Carhartt", "Ralph Lauren", "Calvin Klein", "Tommy Hilfiger",
    56→    "AllSaints", "Mango", "COS", "SSENSE", "Everlane", "Reformation",
    57→    "Shein", "Target", "Walmart", "GOAT", "StockX", "Depop",
    58→    "Poshmark", "Revolve", "Theory", "Vince", "Sandro", "Maje",
    59→    "Acne Studios", "AMI Paris", "A.P.C.", "& Other Stories",
    60→    "Cos", "Anthropologie", "Free People", "Urban Outfitters",
    61→]
    62→
    63→# Multi-currency price pattern: $, €, £, CA$
    64→PRICE_PATTERN = re.compile(r"(?:[$€£]|CA\$)\s?([\d,]+\.?\d{0,2})")
    65→
    66→# Item-price line pattern: "item name - $XX.XX" or "item name $XX.XX" or "item name Price: $XX.XX"
    67→ITEM_PRICE_PATTERN = re.compile(
    68→    r"(?:(?:\d+x?\s+)?(.+?))\s*[-–—]?\s*(?:Price:\s*)?(?:[$€£]|CA\$)\s?([\d,]+\.?\d{0,2})"
    69→)
    70→
    71→# Order status detection
    72→STATUS_PATTERN = re.compile(
    73→    r"(order confirmed|shipped|delivered|out for delivery|in transit)", re.I
    74→)
    75→
    76→# Tracking number extraction
    77→TRACKING_PATTERN = re.compile(
    78→    r"(?:tracking|track)[^\d]*(\b[A-Z0-9]{10,30}\b)", re.I
    79→)
    80→
    81→# Merchant detection from sender domain
    82→MERCHANT_PATTERN = re.compile(r"@([\w.-]+\.\w+)")
    83→
    84→
    85→def _is_receipt(email: dict) -> bool:
    86→    """Check if email is likely a receipt based on sender AND subject."""
    87→    sender = email.get("sender", "").lower()
    88→    subject = email.get("subject", "").lower()
    89→    receipt_keywords = ["order", "receipt", "confirmation", "shipped", "purchase"]
    90→    sender_match = any(pattern in sender for pattern in RECEIPT_SENDERS)
    91→    subject_match = any(kw in subject for kw in receipt_keywords)
    92→    return sender_match and subject_match
    93→
    94→
    95→def _detect_brand(text: str, sender: str) -> str:
    96→    """Detect the most likely brand from text content and sender."""
    97→    for brand in KNOWN_BRANDS:
    98→        if brand.lower() in text.lower() or brand.lower() in sender.lower():
    99→            return brand
   100→    match = re.search(r"@([\w.-]+)", sender)
   101→    if match:
   102→        domain = match.group(1).split(".")[0].capitalize()
   103→        return domain
   104→    return "Unknown"
   105→
   106→
   107→def _detect_merchant(sender: str) -> str | None:
   108→    """Extract merchant name from sender email domain."""
   109→    match = MERCHANT_PATTERN.search(sender)
   110→    if match:
   111→        domain = match.group(1).lower()
   112→        # Map known domains to clean merchant names
   113→        merchant_map = {
   114→            "amazon.com": "Amazon",
   115→            "nordstrom.com": "Nordstrom",
   116→            "macys.com": "Macy's",
   117→            "target.com": "Target",
   118→            "walmart.com": "Walmart",
   119→            "shopify.com": "Shopify",
   120→            "etsy.com": "Etsy",
   121→            "ebay.com": "eBay",
   122→            "ssense.com": "SSENSE",
   123→            "farfetch.com": "Farfetch",
   124→            "net-a-porter.com": "Net-a-Porter",
   125→            "mrporter.com": "Mr Porter",
   126→            "asos.com": "ASOS",
   127→            "revolve.com": "Revolve",
   128→            "stockx.com": "StockX",
   129→            "goat.com": "GOAT",
   130→            "depop.com": "Depop",
   131→            "poshmark.com": "Poshmark",
   132→            "grailed.com": "Grailed",
   133→            "thredup.com": "ThredUp",
   134→            "shein.com": "Shein",
   135→        }
   136→        for pattern, name in merchant_map.items():
   137→            if pattern in domain:
   138→                return name
   139→        # Fallback: capitalize the first part of the domain
   140→        return domain.split(".")[0].capitalize()
   141→    return None
   142→
   143→
   144→def _extract_order_status(text: str) -> str | None:
   145→    """Extract order status from email text."""
   146→    match = STATUS_PATTERN.search(text)
   147→    if match:
   148→        status = match.group(1).lower()
   149→        # Normalize to simple status values
   150→        if "confirmed" in status:
   151→            return "confirmed"
   152→        if "shipped" in status or "in transit" in status:
   153→            return "shipped"
   154→        if "delivered" in status or "out for delivery" in status:
   155→            return "delivered"
   156→    return None
   157→
   158→
   159→def _extract_tracking(text: str) -> str | None:
   160→    """Extract tracking number from email text."""
   161→    match = TRACKING_PATTERN.search(text)
   162→    return match.group(1) if match else None
   163→
   164→
   165→def _parse_price(price_str: str) -> float | None:
   166→    """Parse a price string like '129.99' or '1,299.99' to float."""
   167→    try:
   168→        return float(price_str.replace(",", ""))
   169→    except (ValueError, TypeError):
   170→        return None
   171→
   172→
   173→def _categorize_item(item_name: str) -> str | None:
   174→    """Rough category assignment based on item name keywords."""
   175→    name = item_name.lower()
   176→
   177→    # Exclude false positives from financial/marketing language
   178→    exclusions = [
   179→        "short selling", "short position", "top picks", "top rated",
   180→        "top stories", "top news", "top 10", "top five", "top deal",
   181→        "boot camp", "bootcamp", "bootstrap",
   182→        "jacket cover", "dust jacket",
   183→    ]
   184→    if any(exc in name for exc in exclusions):
   185→        return None
   186→
   187→    categories = {
   188→        "shoes": ["shoe", "sneaker", "boot", "sandal", "air max", "jordan", "runner"],
   189→        "tops": ["shirt", "tee", "top", "blouse", "sweater", "hoodie", "jacket", "blazer", "coat"],
   190→        "bottoms": ["pant", "jean", "short", "skirt", "trouser", "legging"],
   191→        "outerwear": ["jacket", "coat", "parka", "blazer", "vest"],
   192→        "accessories": ["hat", "cap", "belt", "bag", "scarf", "watch", "sunglasses", "jewelry"],
   193→        "dresses": ["dress", "romper", "jumpsuit"],
   194→    }
   195→    for cat, keywords in categories.items():
   196→        if any(kw in name for kw in keywords):
   197→            return cat
   198→    return None
   199→
   200→
   201→def _extract_with_llm(email: dict) -> list[dict]:
   202→    """Use Claude Haiku to extract purchases from hard-to-parse emails.
   203→
   204→    Only called when regex extraction returns 0 items. Uses Haiku for
   205→    fast/cheap extraction (~$0.03 per full scrape session).
   206→    """
   207→    api_key = os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY")
   208→    if not api_key:
   209→        return []
   210→
   211→    subject = email.get("subject", "")
   212→    body = email.get("body", "")[:2000]
   213→
   214→    prompt = (
   215→        "Extract purchase items from this receipt email. "
   216→        "Return a JSON object with a single key \"items\" containing an array. "
   217→        "Each item should have: brand (string), merchant (string or null), "
   218→        "item_name (string), price (number or null), currency (string, default \"USD\"), "
   219→        "order_status (string or null: confirmed/shipped/delivered).\n\n"
   220→        "If this is not a receipt or no items can be extracted, return {\"items\": []}.\n\n"
   221→        f"Subject: {subject}\n\nBody:\n{body}"
   222→    )
   223→
   224→    try:
   225→        client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=api_key)
   226→        message = client.messages.create(
   227→            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
   228→            max_tokens=1024,
   229→            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
   230→        )
   231→        content = message.content[0].text
   232→        data = json.loads(content)
   233→        return data.get("items", [])
   234→    except Exception as e:
   235→        logger.warning("LLM extraction failed: %s", e)
   236→        return []
   237→
   238→
   239→def extract_purchases(email: dict) -> list[dict]:
   240→    """Extract purchase items from a receipt email.
   241→
   242→    Returns list of dicts with: brand, merchant, item_name, category, price, date,
   243→    order_status, tracking_number, receipt_text, source_email_id
   244→    """
   245→    if not _is_receipt(email):
   246→        return []
   247→
   248→    body = email.get("body", "")
   249→    subject = email.get("subject", "")
   250→    sender = email.get("sender", "")
   251→    full_text = f"{subject}\n{body}"
   252→
   253→    brand = _detect_brand(full_text, sender)
   254→    merchant = _detect_merchant(sender)
   255→    order_status = _extract_order_status(full_text)
   256→    tracking_number = _extract_tracking(full_text)
   257→    receipt_text = full_text[:500] if full_text else None
   258→
   259→    # Try to extract item-price pairs from body
   260→    matches = ITEM_PRICE_PATTERN.findall(body)
   261→
   262→    purchases = []
   263→    if matches:
   264→        for item_name, price_str in matches:
   265→            item_name = re.sub(r"^\d+x?\s+", "", item_name.strip()).strip()
   266→            if len(item_name) < 3 or item_name.lower() in ("subtotal", "total", "tax", "shipping"):
   267→                continue
   268→            price = _parse_price(price_str)
   269→            purchases.append({
   270→                "brand": brand,
   271→                "merchant": merchant,
   272→                "item_name": item_name,
   273→                "category": _categorize_item(item_name),
   274→                "price": price,
   275→                "date": email.get("date"),
   276→                "order_status": order_status,
   277→                "tracking_number": tracking_number,
   278→                "receipt_text": receipt_text,
   279→                "source_email_id": email.get("message_id"),
   280→            })
   281→    else:
   282→        # Fallback: extract from subject line — require price + fashion signal
   283→        prices = PRICE_PATTERN.findall(full_text)
   284→        price = _parse_price(prices[0]) if prices else None
   285→        item_name = re.sub(
   286→            r"^(your |order |re: |fwd: |amazon\.com order of )",
   287→            "",
   288→            subject,
   289→            flags=re.IGNORECASE,
   290→        ).strip().rstrip(".")
   291→        # Only store subject-line items that have a price and look like fashion
   292→        _fashion_signals = (
   293→            "shirt", "tee", "top", "blouse", "sweater", "hoodie", "jacket",
   294→            "blazer", "coat", "pant", "jean", "short", "skirt", "trouser",
   295→            "dress", "shoe", "sneaker", "boot", "sandal", "hat", "cap",
   296→            "belt", "bag", "scarf", "watch", "sunglasses", "legging",
   297→            "romper", "jumpsuit", "vest", "parka", "jordan", "air max",
   298→        )
   299→        has_fashion_signal = any(sig in item_name.lower() for sig in _fashion_signals)
   300→        if item_name and price is not None and has_fashion_signal:
   301→            purchases.append({
   302→                "brand": brand,
   303→                "merchant": merchant,
   304→                "item_name": item_name,
   305→                "category": _categorize_item(item_name),
   306→                "price": price,
   307→                "date": email.get("date"),
   308→                "order_status": order_status,
   309→                "tracking_number": tracking_number,
   310→                "receipt_text": receipt_text,
   311→                "source_email_id": email.get("message_id"),
   312→            })
   313→
   314→    # LLM fallback: if regex found nothing, try cheap LLM extraction
   315→    if not purchases:
   316→        llm_items = _extract_with_llm(email)
   317→        for item in llm_items:
   318→            item_name = item.get("item_name", "")
   319→            if not item_name or len(item_name) < 3:
   320→                continue
   321→            purchases.append({
   322→                "brand": item.get("brand") or brand,
   323→                "merchant": item.get("merchant") or merchant,
   324→                "item_name": item_name,
   325→                "category": _categorize_item(item_name),
   326→                "price": item.get("price"),
   327→                "date": email.get("date"),
   328→                "order_status": item.get("order_status") or order_status,
   329→                "tracking_number": tracking_number,
   330→                "receipt_text": receipt_text,
   331→                "source_email_id": email.get("message_id"),
   332→            })
   333→
   334→    return purchases
   335→

1→from uuid import UUID
     2→
     3→from fastapi import FastAPI
     4→from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
     5→import socketio
     6→
     7→from routers import auth, queue, users
     8→from scraper.routes import router as scraper_router
     9→from judges.routes import router as judges_router
    10→from agent.orchestrator import MiraOrchestrator
    11→
    12→sio = socketio.AsyncServer(async_mode="asgi", cors_allowed_origins="*")
    13→
    14→# Map socket IDs to user IDs for disconnect cleanup
    15→_sid_to_user: dict[str, str] = {}
    16→
    17→app = FastAPI(title="Mirrorless API", version="0.1.0")
    18→
    19→# CORS on FastAPI only — Socket.io handles its own CORS via cors_allowed_origins
    20→app.add_middleware(
    21→    CORSMiddleware,
    22→    allow_origins=["*"],
    23→    allow_credentials=True,
    24→    allow_methods=["*"],
    25→    allow_headers=["*"],
    26→)
    27→
    28→app.include_router(auth.router)
    29→app.include_router(queue.router)
    30→app.include_router(users.router)
    31→app.include_router(scraper_router)
    32→app.include_router(judges_router)
    33→
    34→# Make sio and Mira accessible to routes
    35→app.state.sio = sio
    36→mira = MiraOrchestrator(socket_io=sio)
    37→app.state.mira = mira
    38→
    39→
    40→@app.get("/health")
    41→async def health():
    42→    return {"status": "ok"}
    43→
    44→
    45→# --- Socket.io events ---
    46→
    47→
    48→@sio.event
    49→async def connect(sid, environ):
    50→    print(f"[socket] Client connected: {sid}")
    51→
    52→
    53→@sio.event
    54→async def join_room(sid, data):
    55→    """Client joins a user-specific room for targeted events."""
    56→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    57→    if user_id:
    58→        await sio.enter_room(sid, user_id)
    59→        _sid_to_user[sid] = user_id
    60→        print(f"[socket] {sid} joined room {user_id}")
    61→
    62→
    63→@sio.event
    64→async def disconnect(sid):
    65→    print(f"[socket] Client disconnected: {sid}")
    66→    user_id = _sid_to_user.pop(sid, None)
    67→    if user_id and user_id in mira.sessions:
    68→        try:
    69→            await mira.end_session(user_id)
    70→            print(f"[socket] Cleaned up session for {user_id}")
    71→        except Exception as e:
    72→            print(f"[socket] Failed to clean up session for {user_id}: {e}")
    73→
    74→
    75→def _is_valid_uuid(value: str) -> bool:
    76→    try:
    77→        UUID(value)
    78→        return True
    79→    except (ValueError, AttributeError):
    80→        return False
    81→
    82→
    83→@sio.event
    84→async def start_session(sid, data):
    85→    """Start a Mira session for a user."""
    86→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
    87→    if not user_id:
    88→        return
    89→
    90→    if not _is_valid_uuid(user_id):
    91→        print(f"[mira] Invalid UUID from {sid}: {user_id}")
    92→        await sio.emit("session_error", {"error": f"Invalid user ID: must be a valid UUID"}, to=sid)
    93→        return
    94→
    95→    print(f"[mira] Starting session for user {user_id}")
    96→    try:
    97→        await mira.start_session(user_id)
    98→    except Exception as e:
    99→        print(f"[mira] start_session failed for {user_id}: {e}")
   100→        await sio.emit("session_error", {"error": f"Failed to start session: {e}"}, to=sid)
   101→
   102→
   103→@sio.event
   104→async def mirror_event(sid, data):
   105→    """Handle events from the mirror (voice, gesture, pose, snapshot)."""
   106→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   107→    event = data.get("event", {})
   108→    if not user_id or not event:
   109→        return
   110→    try:
   111→        await mira.handle_event(user_id, event)
   112→    except Exception as e:
   113→        print(f"[mira] mirror_event failed for {user_id}: {e}")
   114→        await sio.emit("session_error", {"error": f"Event processing failed: {e}"}, to=sid)
   115→
   116→
   117→@sio.event
   118→async def end_session(sid, data):
   119→    """End a Mira session."""
   120→    user_id = data.get("user_id")
   121→    if not user_id:
   122→        return
   123→    print(f"[mira] Ending session for user {user_id}")
   124→    result = await mira.end_session(user_id)
   125→    if result:
   126→        await sio.emit("session_recap", result, room=user_id)
   127→
   128→
   129→# Wrap FastAPI with Socket.io — no outer CORS wrapper needed
   130→# (Socket.io has its own CORS via cors_allowed_origins, FastAPI has its own middleware)
   131→socket_app = socketio.ASGIApp(sio, other_asgi_app=app)
   132→

content.content:
1→"""Mira — Event-driven AI agent orchestrator.
     2→
     3→Receives events (voice, gestures, poses, silence) and orchestrates Claude API
     4→calls with tool use. Streams responses to HeyGen for voice output and broadcasts
     5→tool results to the frontend via Socket.io.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→import json
    10→import os
    11→import time
    12→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    13→from uuid import uuid4
    14→
    15→import anthropic
    16→from dotenv import load_dotenv
    17→
    18→from agent.prompts import build_system_prompt
    19→from agent.tools import TOOL_DEFINITIONS, execute_tool
    20→from agent.memory import (
    21→    load_user_profile,
    22→    load_user_purchases,
    23→    load_past_sessions,
    24→    save_session_summary,
    25→    get_user_oauth_token,
    26→)
    27→from models.database import NeonHTTPClient
    28→
    29→load_dotenv()
    30→
    31→CLAUDE_MODEL = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"
    32→SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5
    33→EVENT_BATCH_WINDOW_MS = 200
    34→SOFT_API_LIMIT = 20
    35→
    36→
    37→@dataclass
    38→class SessionState:
    39→    """Tracks state for a single mirror session."""
    40→
    41→    session_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid4()))
    42→    user_id: str = ""
    43→    api_calls: int = 0
    44→    items_shown: int = 0
    45→    likes: int = 0
    46→    dislikes: int = 0
    47→    liked_items: list = field(default_factory=list)
    48→    conversation_history: list = field(default_factory=list)
    49→    is_active: bool = True
    50→    last_input_time: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    51→    wrapping_up: bool = False
    52→    is_processing: bool = False
    53→
    54→
    55→class MiraOrchestrator:
    56→    """Event-driven orchestrator for the Mira AI stylist agent."""
    57→
    58→    def __init__(self, socket_io=None):
    59→        self.client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic(api_key=os.getenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"))
    60→        self.sio = socket_io
    61→        self.sessions: dict[str, SessionState] = {}
    62→        self._silence_tasks: dict[str, asyncio.Task] = {}
    63→
    64→    async def start_session(self, user_id: str) -> SessionState:
    65→        """Initialize a new Mira session for a user."""
    66→        # End any existing session to prevent orphaned timers
    67→        if user_id in self.sessions:
    68→            await self.end_session(user_id)
    69→
    70→        session = SessionState(user_id=user_id)
    71→        self.sessions[user_id] = session
    72→
    73→        # Load user data from DB in parallel — fall back to empty defaults if any fail
    74→        profile = {}
    75→        purchases = []
    76→        past_sessions = []
    77→        oauth_token = None
    78→        db = NeonHTTPClient()
    79→        try:
    80→            profile, purchases, past_sessions, oauth_token = await asyncio.gather(
    81→                load_user_profile(db, user_id),
    82→                load_user_purchases(db, user_id),
    83→                load_past_sessions(db, user_id),
    84→                get_user_oauth_token(db, user_id),
    85→            )
    86→        except Exception as e:
    87→            print(f"[mira] Failed to load user data for {user_id}, using defaults: {e}")
    88→        finally:
    89→            await db.close()
    90→
    91→        # Store on session for tool access
    92→        session.user_context = {
    93→            "profile": profile,
    94→            "purchases": purchases,
    95→            "past_sessions": past_sessions,
    96→            "oauth_token": oauth_token,
    97→        }
    98→
    99→        # Build system prompt with all user data
   100→        session.system_prompt = build_system_prompt(
   101→            user_profile=profile,
   102→            purchases=purchases,
   103→            session_history=past_sessions,
   104→            session_state={
   105→                "items_shown": 0,
   106→                "likes": 0,
   107→                "dislikes": 0,
   108→                "api_calls": 0,
   109→            },
   110→        )
   111→
   112→        # Emit system prompt for debugging
   113→        if self.sio:
   114→            await self.sio.emit(
   115→                "debug_system_prompt",
   116→                {"prompt": session.system_prompt},
   117→                room=user_id,
   118→            )
   119→
   120→        # Create the DB session record — continue without persistence on failure
   121→        db = NeonHTTPClient()
   122→        try:
   123→            await db.execute(
   124→                "INSERT INTO sessions (id, user_id, started_at, status) "
   125→                "VALUES ($1, $2, now(), 'active')",
   126→                [session.session_id, user_id],
   127→            )
   128→        except Exception as e:
   129→            print(f"[mira] Failed to insert session record for {user_id}: {e}")
   130→        finally:
   131→            await db.close()
   132→
   133→        # Start silence detection
   134→        self._start_silence_timer(user_id)
   135→
   136→        # Trigger Mira's opening line
   137→        await self.handle_event(user_id, {
   138→            "type": "session_start",
   139→            "message": "A new user just stepped up to the mirror. Introduce yourself and start the session.",
   140→        })
   141→
   142→        return session
   143→
   144→    async def handle_event(self, user_id: str, event: dict) -> None:
   145→        """Handle an incoming event and generate Mira's response.
   146→
   147→        Events are: voice, gesture (swipe_right, swipe_left, thumbs_up, thumbs_down),
   148→        pose, snapshot, silence, session_start.
   149→        """
   150→        session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   151→        if not session or not session.is_active:
   152→            return
   153→
   154→        session.is_processing = True
   155→        try:
   156→            session.last_input_time = time.time()
   157→            self._start_silence_timer(user_id)
   158→
   159→            # Track gesture outcomes
   160→            event_type = event.get("type")
   161→            gesture = event.get("gesture")
   162→
   163→            if gesture == "thumbs_up" or gesture == "swipe_right":
   164→                session.likes += 1
   165→                if hasattr(session, "_last_shown_item") and session._last_shown_item:
   166→                    session.liked_items.append(session._last_shown_item)
   167→            elif gesture == "thumbs_down" or gesture == "swipe_left":
   168→                session.dislikes += 1
   169→
   170→            # Build user message from event
   171→            user_message = self._event_to_message(event)
   172→            session.conversation_history.append({
   173→                "role": "user",
   174→                "content": user_message,
   175→            })
   176→
   177→            # Update system prompt with current session state
   178→            session.system_prompt = build_system_prompt(
   179→                user_profile=session.user_context["profile"],
   180→                purchases=session.user_context["purchases"],
   181→                session_history=session.user_context["past_sessions"],
   182→                session_state={
   183→                    "items_shown": session.items_shown,
   184→                    "likes": session.likes,
   185→                    "dislikes": session.dislikes,
   186→                    "api_calls": session.api_calls,
   187→                },
   188→            )
   189→
   190→            # Call Claude
   191→            await self._call_claude(session)
   192→        finally:
   193→            session.is_processing = False
   194→
   195→    async def _call_claude(self, session: SessionState) -> None:
   196→        """Make a streaming Claude API call with tool use."""
   197→        if session.api_calls >= SOFT_API_LIMIT:
   198→            print(f"[mira] API limit reached for {session.user_id}")
   199→            return
   200→
   201→        session.api_calls += 1
   202→
   203→        # Collect full response (streaming to HeyGen happens via callback)
   204→        collected_text = ""
   205→        tool_uses = []
   206→
   207→        try:
   208→            async with self.client.messages.stream(
   209→                model=CLAUDE_MODEL,
   210→                max_tokens=300,
   211→                system=session.system_prompt,
   212→                messages=session.conversation_history,
   213→                tools=TOOL_DEFINITIONS,
   214→            ) as stream:
   215→                async for event in stream:
   216→                    if event.type == "content_block_delta":
   217→                        if hasattr(event.delta, "text"):
   218→                            collected_text += event.delta.text
   219→                            # Stream each chunk to HeyGen / frontend
   220→                            await self._stream_text(session.user_id, event.delta.text)
   221→
   222→                    elif event.type == "content_block_stop":
   223→                        pass
   224→
   225→                # Get the final message for tool use blocks
   226→                final_message = await stream.get_final_message()
   227→        except Exception as e:
   228→            print(f"[mira] Claude API call failed for {session.user_id}: {e}")
   229→            # Pop the last user message to keep conversation history consistent
   230→            if session.conversation_history and session.conversation_history[-1]["role"] == "user":
   231→                session.conversation_history.pop()
   232→            return
   233→
   234→        # Process any tool use blocks
   235→        for block in final_message.content:
   236→            if block.type == "tool_use":
   237→                tool_uses.append(block)
   238→
   239→        # Add assistant message to history
   240→        session.conversation_history.append({
   241→            "role": "assistant",
   242→            "content": final_message.content,
   243→        })
   244→
   245→        # Handle tool calls
   246→        if tool_uses:
   247→            await self._handle_tool_calls(session, tool_uses)
   248→
   249→    async def _handle_tool_calls(self, session: SessionState, tool_uses: list) -> None:
   250→        """Execute tool calls and continue the conversation."""
   251→        tool_results = []
   252→
   253→        for tool_use in tool_uses:
   254→            try:
   255→                result = await execute_tool(
   256→                    tool_name=tool_use.name,
   257→                    tool_input=tool_use.input,
   258→                    user_context={
   259→                        "user_id": session.user_id,
   260→                        "oauth_token": session.user_context.get("oauth_token"),
   261→                    },
   262→                )
   263→            except Exception as e:
   264→                print(f"[mira] Tool {tool_use.name} failed: {e}")
   265→                result = {"error": f"Tool execution failed: {str(e)}"}
   266→
   267→            # Parallel broadcast: send results to frontend via Socket.io
   268→            frontend_payload = result.pop("frontend_payload", None)
   269→            if frontend_payload and self.sio:
   270→                await self.sio.emit(
   271→                    "tool_result",
   272→                    frontend_payload,
   273→                    room=session.user_id,
   274→                )
   275→
   276→            # Track items shown
   277→            if tool_use.name == "search_clothing" and result.get("results"):
   278→                session.items_shown += len(result["results"])
   279→                if result["results"]:
   280→                    session._last_shown_item = result["results"][0]
   281→
   282→            tool_results.append({
   283→                "type": "tool_result",
   284→                "tool_use_id": tool_use.id,
   285→                "content": json.dumps(result),
   286→            })
   287→
   288→        # Add tool results to conversation and continue
   289→        session.conversation_history.append({
   290→            "role": "user",
   291→            "content": tool_results,
   292→        })
   293→
   294→        # Call Claude again to process tool results
   295→        await self._call_claude(session)
   296→
   297→    async def end_session(self, user_id: str) -> dict | None:
   298→        """End a session and save summary."""
   299→        session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   300→        if not session:
   301→            return None
   302→
   303→        session.is_active = False
   304→        self._cancel_silence_timer(user_id)
   305→
   306→        # Generate session summary via Claude
   307→        try:
   308→            summary = await self._generate_summary(session)
   309→        except Exception as e:
   310→            print(f"[mira] Failed to generate summary for {user_id}: {e}")
   311→            summary = "Session ended before summary could be generated."
   312→
   313→        # Save to DB — wrapped in error handling so a DB failure
   314→        # doesn't prevent the session_ended event from reaching the frontend
   315→        db = NeonHTTPClient()
   316→        try:
   317→            await save_session_summary(
   318→                db=db,
   319→                session_id=session.session_id,
   320→                summary=summary,
   321→                liked_items=session.liked_items,
   322→                reactions={
   323→                    "likes": session.likes,
   324→                    "dislikes": session.dislikes,
   325→                    "items_shown": session.items_shown,
   326→                },
   327→            )
   328→        except Exception as e:
   329→            print(f"[mira] Failed to save session summary: {e}")
   330→        finally:
   331→            await db.close()
   332→
   333→        # Emit session end to frontend
   334→        if self.sio:
   335→            await self.sio.emit(
   336→                "session_ended",
   337→                {
   338→                    "session_id": session.session_id,
   339→                    "summary": summary,
   340→                    "liked_items": session.liked_items,
   341→                    "stats": {
   342→                        "items_shown": session.items_shown,
   343→                        "likes": session.likes,
   344→                        "dislikes": session.dislikes,
   345→                    },
   346→                },
   347→                room=user_id,
   348→            )
   349→
   350→        del self.sessions[user_id]
   351→        return {"summary": summary, "liked_items": session.liked_items}
   352→
   353→    async def _generate_summary(self, session: SessionState) -> str:
   354→        """Ask Claude to generate a short session summary for memory."""
   355→        response = await self.client.messages.create(
   356→            model=CLAUDE_MODEL,
   357→            max_tokens=200,
   358→            system="Summarize this styling session in 2-3 sentences for future reference. Include key style preferences discovered, items liked, and overall vibe.",
   359→            messages=[
   360→                {
   361→                    "role": "user",
   362→                    "content": f"Session had {session.items_shown} items shown, {session.likes} liked, {session.dislikes} disliked. Liked items: {json.dumps(session.liked_items[:5])}",
   363→                }
   364→            ],
   365→        )
   366→        return response.content[0].text
   367→
   368→    def _event_to_message(self, event: dict) -> str | list:
   369→        """Convert an event dict to a Claude user message."""
   370→        event_type = event.get("type")
   371→
   372→        if event_type == "voice":
   373→            return event.get("transcript", "")
   374→
   375→        elif event_type == "gesture":
   376→            gesture = event.get("gesture", "unknown")
   377→            gesture_descriptions = {
   378→                "swipe_right": "The user swiped right (like/next).",
   379→                "swipe_left": "The user swiped left (dislike/skip).",
   380→                "thumbs_up": "The user gave a thumbs up (like this item).",
   381→                "thumbs_down": "The user gave a thumbs down (dislike this item).",
   382→            }
   383→            return gesture_descriptions.get(gesture, f"The user made a {gesture} gesture.")
   384→
   385→        elif event_type == "pose":
   386→            return [
   387→                {"type": "text", "text": "The user struck a new pose. Here's what they look like:"},
   388→                {
   389→                    "type": "image",
   390→                    "source": {
   391→                        "type": "base64",
   392→                        "media_type": "image/jpeg",
   393→                        "data": event.get("image_base64", ""),
   394→                    },
   395→                },
   396→            ]
   397→
   398→        elif event_type == "snapshot":
   399→            return [
   400→                {"type": "text", "text": "Here's a snapshot of the user at the mirror:"},
   401→                {
   402→                    "type": "image",
   403→                    "source": {
   404→                        "type": "base64",
   405→                        "media_type": "image/jpeg",
   406→                        "data": event.get("image_base64", ""),
   407→                    },
   408→                },
   409→            ]
   410→
   411→        elif event_type == "silence":
   412→            duration = event.get("duration_seconds", SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
   413→            return f"The user has been silent for {duration} seconds. Say something to keep the conversation going."
   414→
   415→        elif event_type == "session_start":
   416→            return event.get("message", "Start the session.")
   417→
   418→        return str(event)
   419→
   420→    async def _stream_text(self, user_id: str, text_chunk: str) -> None:
   421→        """Stream a text chunk to the frontend and HeyGen."""
   422→        if self.sio:
   423→            await self.sio.emit(
   424→                "mira_speech",
   425→                {"text": text_chunk, "is_chunk": True},
   426→                room=user_id,
   427→            )
   428→
   429→    def _start_silence_timer(self, user_id: str) -> None:
   430→        """Start or restart the silence detection timer."""
   431→        self._cancel_silence_timer(user_id)
   432→
   433→        async def _silence_callback():
   434→            await asyncio.sleep(SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
   435→            session = self.sessions.get(user_id)
   436→            if session and session.is_active and not session.is_processing:
   437→                await self.handle_event(user_id, {
   438→                    "type": "silence",
   439→                    "duration_seconds": SILENCE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
   440→                })
   441→
   442→        self._silence_tasks[user_id] = asyncio.create_task(_silence_callback())
   443→
   444→    def _cancel_silence_timer(self, user_id: str) -> None:
   445→        """Cancel the silence timer for a user."""
   446→        task = self._silence_tasks.pop(user_id, None)
   447→        if task and not task.done():
   448→            task.cancel()
   449→

1→"""Pydantic models for API request/response validation."""
     2→
     3→from datetime import date, datetime
     4→from typing import Optional
     5→from uuid import UUID
     6→
     7→from pydantic import BaseModel, EmailStr
     8→
     9→
    10→class UserCreate(BaseModel):
    11→    name: str
    12→    email: str
    13→    phone: Optional[str] = None
    14→    poke_id: Optional[str] = None
    15→
    16→
    17→class UserResponse(BaseModel):
    18→    id: UUID
    19→    name: str
    20→    email: str
    21→    phone: Optional[str]
    22→    poke_id: Optional[str]
    23→    created_at: datetime
    24→
    25→
    26→class StyleProfileUpdate(BaseModel):
    27→    brands: list[str] = []
    28→    price_range: Optional[dict] = None
    29→    style_tags: list[str] = []
    30→    size_info: Optional[dict] = None
    31→    narrative_summary: Optional[str] = None
    32→
    33→
    34→class StyleProfileResponse(BaseModel):
    35→    user_id: UUID
    36→    brands: list[str]
    37→    price_range: Optional[dict]
    38→    style_tags: list[str]
    39→    size_info: Optional[dict]
    40→    narrative_summary: Optional[str]
    41→
    42→
    43→class PurchaseCreate(BaseModel):
    44→    brand: str
    45→    item_name: str
    46→    category: Optional[str] = None
    47→    price: Optional[float] = None
    48→    date: Optional[date] = None
    49→    source_email_id: Optional[str] = None
    50→
    51→
    52→class PurchaseResponse(BaseModel):
    53→    id: UUID
    54→    user_id: UUID

1→"""Database connection management.
     2→
     3→Supports two modes:
     4→- asyncpg pool: Used when port 5432 is reachable (production on Render)
     5→- Neon serverless HTTP: Used when port 5432 is blocked (local dev)
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import os
     9→from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
    10→from urllib.parse import urlparse
    11→
    12→import httpx
    13→from dotenv import load_dotenv
    14→
    15→load_dotenv()
    16→
    17→DATABASE_URL = os.getenv("DATABASE_URL", "")
    18→
    19→
    20→def _get_neon_http_url() -> str:
    21→    """Extract the HTTPS SQL endpoint from the DATABASE_URL."""
    22→    parsed = urlparse(DATABASE_URL)
    23→    return f"https://{parsed.hostname}/sql"
    24→
    25→
    26→class NeonHTTPClient:
    27→    """Thin wrapper around Neon's serverless HTTP SQL API."""
    28→
    29→    def __init__(self, connection_string: str | None = None):
    30→        self.connection_string = connection_string or DATABASE_URL
    31→        parsed = urlparse(self.connection_string)
    32→        self.api_url = f"https://{parsed.hostname}/sql"
    33→        self._client = httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=30)
    34→
    35→    async def execute(self, query: str, params: list | None = None) -> list[dict]:
    36→        """Execute a SQL query and return rows as dicts."""
    37→        resp = await self._client.post(
    38→            self.api_url,
    39→            json={"query": query, "params": params or []},
    40→            headers={
    41→                "Content-Type": "application/json",
    42→                "Neon-Connection-String": self.connection_string,
    43→            },
    44→        )
    45→        resp.raise_for_status()
    46→        data = resp.json()
    47→        return data.get("rows", [])
    48→
    49→    async def fetchval(self, query: str, params: list | None = None):
    50→        """Execute a query and return the first column of the first row."""
    51→        rows = await self.execute(query, params)
    52→        if rows:
    53→            first_row = rows[0]
    54→            return list(first_row.values())[0]
    55→        return None
    56→
    57→    async def close(self):
    58→        await self._client.aclose()
    59→
    60→
    61→async def get_neon_client() -> NeonHTTPClient:
    62→    """Create a NeonHTTPClient instance."""
    63→    return NeonHTTPClient()
    64→
    65→
    66→# For production (Render), use asyncpg pool
    67→async def get_pool():
    68→    """Create and return an asyncpg connection pool. Use in production."""
    69→    import asyncpg
    70→
    71→    return await asyncpg.create_pool(
    72→        DATABASE_URL,
    73→        min_size=2,
    74→        max_size=10,
    75→        ssl="require",
    76→    )
    77→
    78→
    79→@asynccontextmanager
    80→async def get_connection(pool):
    81→    """Acquire a connection from an asyncpg pool."""
    82→    import asyncpg
    83→
    84→    conn = await pool.acquire()
    85→    try:
    86→        yield conn
    87→    finally:
    88→        await pool.release(conn)
    89→

1→"""FastAPI routes for the scraping pipeline."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→
     5→from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException, Request
     6→from pydantic import BaseModel
     7→
     8→from models.database import get_neon_client
     9→from scraper.gmail_auth import exchange_auth_code
    10→from scraper.pipeline import fast_scrape
    11→from scraper.profile_builder import build_style_profile
    12→from scraper.brand_scanner import scan_brand_frequency
    13→from scraper.db import (
    14→    store_purchases, store_style_profile, get_user_token, store_user_token,
    15→    get_last_scraped_at, set_last_scraped_at, get_all_purchases,
    16→)
    17→from scraper.socket_events import emit_purchase_found, emit_scrape_progress, emit_scrape_complete
    18→
    19→router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/scrape", tags=["scraping"])
    20→
    21→
    22→class AuthRequest(BaseModel):
    23→    user_id: str
    24→    auth_code: str
    25→    redirect_uri: str
    26→
    27→
    28→class ScrapeRequest(BaseModel):
    29→    user_id: str
    30→
    31→
    32→@router.post("/auth")
    33→async def exchange_token(req: AuthRequest):
    34→    """Exchange Google OAuth auth code for tokens and store them."""
    35→    try:
    36→        token_data = exchange_auth_code(req.auth_code, req.redirect_uri)
    37→    except Exception as e:
    38→        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"OAuth exchange failed: {e}")
    39→
    40→    db = await get_neon_client()
    41→    try:
    42→        await store_user_token(db, req.user_id, token_data)
    43→    finally:
    44→        await db.close()
    45→
    46→    return {"status": "ok"}
    47→
    48→
    49→@router.post("/start")
    50→async def start_scrape(req: ScrapeRequest, request: Request):
    51→    """Kick off streaming scrape in background. Results arrive via Socket.io."""
    52→    db = await get_neon_client()
    53→    try:
    54→        token_data = await get_user_token(db, req.user_id)
    55→        if not token_data:
    56→            raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="No OAuth token for user. Complete /auth first.")
    57→    finally:
    58→        await db.close()
    59→
    60→    sio = request.app.state.sio
    61→    asyncio.create_task(_background_scrape(req.user_id, token_data, sio))
    62→
    63→    return {"status": "started"}
    64→
    65→
    66→async def _background_scrape(user_id: str, token_data: dict, sio):
    67→    """Run scrape in background, streaming results via Socket.io.
    68→
    69→    Incremental: if the user has been scraped before, only fetches emails
    70→    newer than last_scraped_at. Profile is always rebuilt from ALL purchases.
    71→    """
    72→    db = await get_neon_client()
    73→    try:
    74→        await emit_scrape_progress(sio, user_id, 0, [], "searching")
    75→
    76→        # Check for previous scrape timestamp
    77→        last_scraped = await get_last_scraped_at(db, user_id)
    78→
    79→        total_count = [0]  # mutable counter for closure
    80→
    81→        async def on_email(email, purchases):
    82→            total_count[0] += len(purchases)
    83→            # Store each batch immediately
    84→            await store_purchases(db, user_id, purchases)
    85→            # Stream to frontend
    86→            await emit_purchase_found(
    87→                sio, user_id,
    88→                email_subject=email.get("subject", ""),
    89→                purchases=purchases,
    90→                total_so_far=total_count[0],
    91→            )
    92→
    93→        result = await fast_scrape(token_data, on_email=on_email, since=last_scraped)
    94→
    95→        # Mark scrape timestamp before profile rebuild
    96→        await set_last_scraped_at(db, user_id)
    97→
    98→        # Rebuild profile from ALL purchases (old + new) for full accuracy
    99→        all_purchases = await get_all_purchases(db, user_id)
   100→        profile = build_style_profile(all_purchases, result.brand_freq)
   101→
   102→        # Store final profile
   103→        await store_style_profile(db, user_id, profile)
   104→
   105→        # Emit completion with profile
   106→        await emit_scrape_complete(sio, user_id, profile)
   107→
   108→    except Exception as e:
   109→        print(f"[scrape] Error for user {user_id}: {e}")
   110→        await emit_scrape_progress(sio, user_id, 0, [], "error")
   111→    finally:
   112→        await db.close()
   113→

1→"""Scraping pipeline: full-history streaming scrape with incremental support."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
     5→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
     6→from datetime import datetime
     7→from functools import partial
     8→
     9→from scraper.gmail_auth import build_gmail_service
    10→from scraper.gmail_fetch import search_emails, get_message_content
    11→from scraper.purchase_parser import extract_purchases
    12→from scraper.brand_scanner import scan_brand_frequency
    13→from scraper.profile_builder import build_style_profile
    14→
    15→# Gmail search queries — full history (no date limits)
    16→RECEIPT_QUERIES = [
    17→    "subject:(order confirmation)",
    18→    "subject:(order shipped)",
    19→    "subject:(your order)",
    20→    "subject:(receipt from)",
    21→    "subject:(purchase confirmed)",
    22→    "from:(noreply OR no-reply OR orders@ OR receipt@) subject:(order OR receipt OR confirmation)",
    23→    "subject:(subscription) category:updates",
    24→]
    25→
    26→BRAND_SCAN_QUERY = "category:promotions newer_than:12m"
    27→
    28→_executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4)
    29→
    30→
    31→@dataclass
    32→class ScrapeResult:
    33→    purchases: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
    34→    brand_freq: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
    35→    profile: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    36→
    37→
    38→def _fetch_receipts_sync(token_data: dict, queries: list[str], max_per_query: int = 20) -> list[dict]:
    39→    """Synchronous: search + fetch receipt emails."""
    40→    service = build_gmail_service(token_data)
    41→    all_message_ids = set()
    42→    for query in queries:
    43→        ids = search_emails(service, query=query, max_results=max_per_query)
    44→        all_message_ids.update(ids)
    45→
    46→    emails = []
    47→    for msg_id in all_message_ids:
    48→        try:
    49→            content = get_message_content(service, msg_id)
    50→            emails.append(content)
    51→        except Exception:
    52→            continue
    53→    return emails
    54→
    55→
    56→def _fetch_subjects_sync(token_data: dict, query: str, max_results: int = 100) -> list[str]:
    57→    """Synchronous: fetch subject lines for brand scanning."""
    58→    service = build_gmail_service(token_data)
    59→    msg_ids = search_emails(service, query=query, max_results=max_results)
    60→    subjects = []
    61→    for msg_id in msg_ids:
    62→        try:
    63→            content = get_message_content(service, msg_id)
    64→            subjects.append(content["subject"])
    65→        except Exception:
    66→            continue
    67→    return subjects
    68→
    69→
    70→def _apply_since(queries: list[str], since: datetime | None) -> list[str]:
    71→    """Append `after:YYYY/MM/DD` to each query if a since date is provided."""
    72→    if not since:
    73→        return queries
    74→    date_filter = f" after:{since.strftime('%Y/%m/%d')}"
    75→    return [q + date_filter for q in queries]
    76→
    77→
    78→async def fast_scrape(token_data: dict, on_email=None, since: datetime | None = None) -> ScrapeResult:
    79→    """Streaming scrape. If `since` is set, only fetches emails after that date."""
    80→    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
    81→
    82→    queries = _apply_since(RECEIPT_QUERIES, since)
    83→
    84→    # Run receipt fetch and brand scan in parallel using thread pool
    85→    # Each thread builds its own Gmail service to avoid httplib2 thread-safety issues
    86→    receipt_task = loop.run_in_executor(
    87→        _executor,
    88→        partial(_fetch_receipts_sync, token_data, queries, 20),
    89→    )
    90→    brand_task = loop.run_in_executor(
    91→        _executor,
    92→        partial(_fetch_subjects_sync, token_data, BRAND_SCAN_QUERY, 100),
    93→    )
    94→
    95→    emails, subjects = await asyncio.gather(receipt_task, brand_task)
    96→
    97→    # Parse purchases from receipt emails, streaming per-email via callback
    98→    all_purchases = []
    99→    for email in emails:
   100→        new_purchases = extract_purchases(email)
   101→        if new_purchases:
   102→            all_purchases.extend(new_purchases)
   103→            if on_email:
   104→                await on_email(email, new_purchases)
   105→
   106→    # Scan brand frequency
   107→    brand_freq = scan_brand_frequency(subjects)
   108→
   109→    # Build profile
   110→    profile = build_style_profile(all_purchases, brand_freq)
   111→
   112→    return ScrapeResult(
   113→        purchases=all_purchases,
   114→        brand_freq=brand_freq,
   115→        profile=profile,
   116→    )
   117→

1→"""Database operations for the scraping pipeline."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→from datetime import datetime, timezone
     5→
     6→
     7→async def store_purchases(db, user_id: str, purchases: list[dict]) -> None:
     8→    """Insert parsed purchases into the purchases table.
     9→
    10→    Uses ON CONFLICT to skip duplicates (same user + email + item).
    11→    """
    12→    for p in purchases:
    13→        receipt_text = p.get("receipt_text")
    14→        if receipt_text and len(receipt_text) > 500:
    15→            receipt_text = receipt_text[:500]
    16→        await db.execute(
    17→            "INSERT INTO purchases "
    18→            "(user_id, brand, item_name, category, price, date, source_email_id, "
    19→            "merchant, order_status, tracking_number, receipt_text) "
    20→            "VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5, $6::date, $7, $8, $9, $10, $11) "
    21→            "ON CONFLICT (user_id, COALESCE(source_email_id, ''), item_name) DO NOTHING",
    22→            [
    23→                user_id,
    24→                p["brand"],
    25→                p["item_name"],
    26→                p.get("category"),
    27→                p.get("price"),
    28→                p.get("date"),
    29→                p.get("source_email_id"),
    30→                p.get("merchant"),
    31→                p.get("order_status"),
    32→                p.get("tracking_number"),
    33→                receipt_text,
    34→            ],
    35→        )
    36→
    37→
    38→async def store_style_profile(db, user_id: str, profile: dict) -> None:
    39→    """Upsert a style profile for the user."""
    40→    await db.execute(
    41→        "INSERT INTO style_profiles (user_id, brands, price_range, style_tags, narrative_summary) "
    42→        "VALUES ($1, $2, $3::jsonb, $4, $5) "
    43→        "ON CONFLICT (user_id) DO UPDATE SET "
    44→        "brands = EXCLUDED.brands, "
    45→        "price_range = EXCLUDED.price_range, "
    46→        "style_tags = EXCLUDED.style_tags, "
    47→        "narrative_summary = EXCLUDED.narrative_summary",
    48→        [
    49→            user_id,
    50→            "{" + ",".join(profile["brands"]) + "}" if profile["brands"] else "{}",
    51→            json.dumps(profile["price_range"]),
    52→            "{" + ",".join(profile["style_tags"]) + "}" if profile["style_tags"] else "{}",
    53→            profile.get("narrative_summary"),
    54→        ],
    55→    )
    56→
    57→
    58→async def get_user_token(db, user_id: str) -> dict | None:
    59→    """Fetch the Google OAuth token for a user."""
    60→    rows = await db.execute(
    61→        "SELECT google_oauth_token FROM users WHERE id = $1",
    62→        [user_id],
    63→    )
    64→    if rows and rows[0].get("google_oauth_token"):
    65→        token = rows[0]["google_oauth_token"]
    66→        return token if isinstance(token, dict) else json.loads(token)
    67→    return None
    68→
    69→
    70→async def store_user_token(db, user_id: str, token_data: dict) -> None:
    71→    """Store Google OAuth token for a user."""
    72→    await db.execute(
    73→        "UPDATE users SET google_oauth_token = $1::jsonb WHERE id = $2",
    74→        [json.dumps(token_data), user_id],
    75→    )
    76→
    77→
    78→async def get_last_scraped_at(db, user_id: str) -> datetime | None:
    79→    """Fetch the last scrape timestamp for a user."""
    80→    rows = await db.execute(
    81→        "SELECT last_scraped_at FROM users WHERE id = $1",
    82→        [user_id],
    83→    )
    84→    if rows and rows[0].get("last_scraped_at"):
    85→        val = rows[0]["last_scraped_at"]
    86→        if isinstance(val, datetime):
    87→            return val
    88→        return datetime.fromisoformat(val)
    89→    return None
    90→
    91→
    92→async def set_last_scraped_at(db, user_id: str) -> None:
    93→    """Set the last scrape timestamp to now."""
    94→    await db.execute(
    95→        "UPDATE users SET last_scraped_at = now() WHERE id = $1",
    96→        [user_id],
    97→    )
    98→
    99→
   100→async def get_all_purchases(db, user_id: str) -> list[dict]:
   101→    """Fetch all purchases for a user (for profile rebuilding after incremental scrape)."""
   102→    rows = await db.execute(
   103→        "SELECT brand, item_name, category, price, date, merchant, order_status "
   104→        "FROM purchases WHERE user_id = $1",
   105→        [user_id],
   106→    )
   107→    return rows or []
   108→

content.content:
1→"""Build a style profile from purchase data and brand frequencies."""
     2→
     3→from collections import Counter
     4→
     5→from agent.prompts import _filter_fashion_purchases
     6→
     7→# Map categories to style tags
     8→CATEGORY_STYLE_MAP = {
     9→    "shoes": ["sneakerhead", "athletic"],
    10→    "tops": ["casual"],
    11→    "bottoms": ["casual"],
    12→    "outerwear": ["layered", "polished"],
    13→    "accessories": ["accessorized"],
    14→    "dresses": ["feminine", "occasion-ready"],
    15→}
    16→
    17→# Map price ranges to style tags
    18→PRICE_STYLE_MAP = [
    19→    (0, 50, "budget-friendly"),
    20→    (50, 150, "mid-range"),
    21→    (150, 500, "premium"),
    22→    (500, float("inf"), "luxury"),
    23→]
    24→
    25→
    26→def build_style_profile(
    27→    purchases: list[dict],
    28→    brand_freq: dict[str, int],
    29→) -> dict:
    30→    """Aggregate purchases and brand frequencies into a style profile.
    31→
    32→    Returns dict matching StyleProfileUpdate schema:
    33→    {brands, price_range, style_tags, narrative_summary}
    34→    """
    35→    # Filter to fashion-only items before building profile
    36→    purchases = _filter_fashion_purchases(purchases)
    37→
    38→    if not purchases and not brand_freq:
    39→        return {
    40→            "brands": [],
    41→            "price_range": {"min": 0, "max": 0, "avg": 0},
    42→            "style_tags": [],
    43→            "narrative_summary": None,
    44→        }
    45→
    46→    # Brands: merge from purchases + frequency scan, ordered by frequency
    47→    brand_counts = Counter(brand_freq)
    48→    for p in purchases:
    49→        brand_counts[p["brand"]] += 1
    50→    brands = [b for b, _ in brand_counts.most_common()]
    51→
    52→    # Price range
    53→    prices = [p["price"] for p in purchases if p.get("price") is not None]
    54→    price_range = {
    55→        "min": min(prices) if prices else 0,
    56→        "max": max(prices) if prices else 0,
    57→        "avg": round(sum(prices) / len(prices), 2) if prices else 0,
    58→    }
    59→
    60→    # Style tags from categories
    61→    style_tags = set()
    62→    category_counts = Counter(p.get("category") for p in purchases if p.get("category"))
    63→    for cat, count in category_counts.items():
    64→        if cat in CATEGORY_STYLE_MAP:
    65→            style_tags.update(CATEGORY_STYLE_MAP[cat])
    66→
    67→    # Style tags from price range
    68→    avg_price = price_range["avg"]
    69→    for low, high, tag in PRICE_STYLE_MAP:
    70→        if low <= avg_price < high:
    71→            style_tags.add(tag)
    72→            break
    73→
    74→    # Narrative summary
    75→    top_brands = ", ".join(brands[:3]) if brands else "various brands"
    76→    cat_summary = ", ".join(
    77→        f"{cat} ({count})" for cat, count in category_counts.most_common(3)
    78→    )
    79→    narrative = (
    80→        f"Shops primarily at {top_brands}. "
    81→        f"Most purchased categories: {cat_summary or 'mixed'}. "
    82→        f"Typical spending: ${price_range['avg']:.0f} per item "
    83→        f"(range ${price_range['min']:.0f}-${price_range['max']:.0f})."
    84→    )
    85→
    86→    return {
    87→        "brands": brands,
    88→        "price_range": price_range,
    89→        "style_tags": sorted(style_tags),
    90→        "narrative_summary": narrative,
    91→    }
    92→

1→"""Scan email subject lines for brand frequency."""
     2→
     3→from collections import Counter
     4→from scraper.purchase_parser import KNOWN_BRANDS
     5→
     6→
     7→def scan_brand_frequency(subjects: list[str]) -> dict[str, int]:
     8→    """Count how often known brands appear in a list of email subjects.
     9→
    10→    Returns dict of {brand: count}, sorted by count descending.
    11→    Only includes brands with at least 1 match.
    12→    """
    13→    counts: Counter = Counter()
    14→    for subject in subjects:
    15→        subject_lower = subject.lower()
    16→        for brand in KNOWN_BRANDS:
    17→            if brand.lower() in subject_lower:
    18→                counts[brand] += 1
    19→    return dict(counts.most_common())
    20→

1→"""Socket.io events for scraping progress updates."""
     2→
     3→
     4→async def emit_purchase_found(
     5→    sio,
     6→    user_id: str,
     7→    email_subject: str,
     8→    purchases: list[dict],
     9→    total_so_far: int,
    10→) -> None:
    11→    """Emit a per-email purchase event as each email is parsed."""

    13→        "purchase_found",

    18→            "total_so_far": total_so_far,
    19→        },

    25→    sio,
    26→    user_id: str,
    27→    purchases_count: int,
    28→    brands_found: list[str],
    29→    phase: str,
    30→) -> None:

1→"""Session memory for Mira — persistent across sessions."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→
     5→from models.database import NeonHTTPClient
     6→
     7→
     8→async def save_session_summary(
     9→    db: NeonHTTPClient,
    10→    session_id: str,
    11→    summary: str,
    12→    liked_items: list[dict],
    13→    reactions: dict,
    14→) -> None:
    15→    """Save a session summary for future Mira context."""
    16→    await db.execute(
    17→        "UPDATE sessions SET ended_at = now(), status = 'completed' WHERE id = $1",
    18→        [session_id],
    19→    )
    20→    # Store summary as outfit_data on a special "summary" record
    21→    await db.execute(
    22→        "INSERT INTO session_outfits (session_id, outfit_data, reaction) "
    23→        "VALUES ($1, $2::jsonb, 'summary')",
    24→        [
    25→            session_id,
    26→            json.dumps({
    27→                "summary": summary,
    28→                "liked_items": liked_items,
    29→                "reactions": reactions,
    30→            }),
    31→        ],
    32→    )
    33→
    34→
    35→async def load_past_sessions(db: NeonHTTPClient, user_id: str) -> list[dict]:
    36→    """Load past session summaries for a user."""
    37→    rows = await db.execute(
    38→        "SELECT so.outfit_data FROM session_outfits so "
    39→        "JOIN sessions s ON so.session_id = s.id "
    40→        "WHERE s.user_id = $1 AND so.reaction = 'summary' "
    41→        "ORDER BY s.ended_at DESC LIMIT 3",
    42→        [user_id],
    43→    )
    44→    results = []
    45→    for row in rows:
    46→        data = row.get("outfit_data", {})
    47→        if isinstance(data, str):
    48→            data = json.loads(data)
    49→        results.append(data)
    50→    return results
    51→
    52→
    53→async def load_user_profile(db: NeonHTTPClient, user_id: str) -> dict:
    54→    """Load user profile and style data in a single query."""
    55→    rows = await db.execute(
    56→        "SELECT u.id, u.name, u.email, "
    57→        "sp.brands, sp.price_range, sp.style_tags, sp.size_info, sp.narrative_summary "
    58→        "FROM users u "
    59→        "LEFT JOIN style_profiles sp ON sp.user_id = u.id "
    60→        "WHERE u.id = $1",
    61→        [user_id],
    62→    )
    63→    if not rows:
    64→        return {}
    65→
    66→    user = rows[0]
    67→    style = user  # All columns are in the same row now
    68→
    69→    # Parse array fields from postgres format
    70→    brands = style.get("brands", [])
    71→    if isinstance(brands, str):
    72→        brands = [b.strip() for b in brands.strip("{}").split(",") if b.strip()]
    73→
    74→    style_tags = style.get("style_tags", [])
    75→    if isinstance(style_tags, str):
    76→        style_tags = [t.strip() for t in style_tags.strip("{}").split(",") if t.strip()]
    77→
    78→    price_range = style.get("price_range")
    79→    if isinstance(price_range, str):
    80→        price_range = json.loads(price_range)
    81→
    82→    return {
    83→        "user_id": str(user["id"]),
    84→        "name": user.get("name", ""),
    85→        "email": user.get("email", ""),
    86→        "brands": brands,
    87→        "price_range": price_range or {},
    88→        "style_tags": style_tags,
    89→        "narrative_summary": style.get("narrative_summary", ""),
    90→    }
    91→
    92→
    93→async def load_user_purchases(db: NeonHTTPClient, user_id: str) -> list[dict]:
    94→    """Load user's purchase history."""
    95→    rows = await db.execute(
    96→        "SELECT brand, item_name, category, price, date "
    97→        "FROM purchases WHERE user_id = $1 "
    98→        "ORDER BY date DESC LIMIT 50",
    99→        [user_id],
   100→    )
   101→    return [
   102→        {
   103→            "brand": r.get("brand", ""),
   104→            "item_name": r.get("item_name", ""),
   105→            "category": r.get("category"),
   106→            "price": float(r["price"]) if r.get("price") else None,
   107→            "date": str(r["date"]) if r.get("date") else None,
   108→        }
   109→        for r in rows
   110→    ]
   111→
   112→
   113→async def get_user_oauth_token(db: NeonHTTPClient, user_id: str) -> dict | None:
   114→    """Get user's OAuth token for Gmail access."""
   115→    rows = await db.execute(
   116→        "SELECT google_oauth_token FROM users WHERE id = $1",
   117→        [user_id],
   118→    )
   119→    if rows and rows[0].get("google_oauth_token"):
   120→        token = rows[0]["google_oauth_token"]
   121→        return token if isinstance(token, dict) else json.loads(token)
   122→    return None
   123→

1→"""Auth endpoints: Google OAuth exchange and profile update."""
     2→
     3→from fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException
     4→from pydantic import BaseModel
     5→
     6→from models.database import NeonHTTPClient
     7→from services.auth import exchange_google_code, upsert_user
     8→
     9→router = APIRouter(prefix="/auth", tags=["auth"])
    10→
    11→
    12→class GoogleAuthRequest(BaseModel):
    13→    code: str
    14→    redirect_uri: str = "postmessage"
    15→
    16→
    17→class ProfileUpdateRequest(BaseModel):
    18→    user_id: str
    19→    name: str
    20→    phone: str
    21→
    22→
    23→@router.post("/google")
    24→async def google_login(body: GoogleAuthRequest):
    25→    """Exchange a Google auth code for tokens, upsert user, return profile."""
    26→    db = NeonHTTPClient()
    27→    try:
    28→        token_data = await exchange_google_code(body.code, body.redirect_uri)
    29→        user = await upsert_user(db, token_data)
    30→        return user
    31→    except Exception as exc:
    32→        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(exc))
    33→    finally:
    34→        await db.close()
    35→
    36→
    37→@router.post("/profile")
    38→async def update_profile(body: ProfileUpdateRequest):
    39→    """Update user name and phone number."""
    40→    db = NeonHTTPClient()
    41→    try:
    42→        rows = await db.execute(
    43→            """
    44→            UPDATE users SET name = $1, phone = $2
    45→            WHERE id = $3::uuid
    46→            RETURNING id, name, email, phone, poke_id
    47→            """,
    48→            [body.name, body.phone, body.user_id],
    49→        )
    50→        if not rows:
    51→            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="User not found")
    52→        return rows[0]
    53→    finally:
    54→        await db.close()
    55→
